name: Lindell Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Lindell Bank is an independent community bank established in 1923, offering full-service banking with 15 locations and 17 ATMs across the St. Louis metro area.
description_long:
Lindell Bank & Trust Company was founded in 1923 and has operated as a locally owned and independent community bank in the St. Louis metropolitan region for over a century. The institution maintains 15 convenient banking locations and 17 ATMs throughout the area, positioning itself as a full-service bank with deep community roots. According to their marketing materials, the bank maintains one of the highest financial ratings in St. Louis for stability and strength.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services including checking accounts (marketed as "No Strings Checking"), savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit with competitive rates (7-month at 3.80% APY, 9-month at 3.40% APY, and 12-month options), online banking, merchant credit card services, mortgage lending for home purchases and refinancing, and eStatement services. They also provide identity theft protection education and scam prevention resources through their education center, reflecting a focus on consumer security awareness.

Lindell Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personal service and community involvement. The bank has announced significant community investments, including a $2.55 million grant for affordable housing development in North St. Louis through partnership with Justine Petersen Housing & Reinvestment Corp. They promote free eStatements, no-fee checking options, and educational resources on their website, including online security and safety videos. Their marketing positions them as offering a personal touch not found at larger institutions.

As a traditional community bank, Lindell Bank's primary strength lies in local market presence and relationship-based banking. However, the website provides limited information about fee structures, loan products beyond mortgages, specific interest rates for checking/savings accounts, minimum balance requirements, or technological capabilities compared to larger regional or national banks. Consumers should directly contact the bank to understand their full product offerings, pricing, and whether they meet specific financial needs. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Scam alert and fraud prevention education
Online security and safety video education center
Mobile banking app (available on Google Play and Apple App Store)
Trust services (implied by bank name, details not provided)
pros: Established 1923 with 100+ years of community banking history and local market presence
15 branch locations and 17 ATMs throughout St. Louis metro area for convenient access
Competitive CD rates (3.80% APY for 7-month, 3.40% APY for 9-month certificates)
Free eStatements service with early delivery, reducing identity theft risk from mail
No-strings checking accounts with no monthly fees advertised on homepage
Community-focused lending, including $2.55 million affordable housing grants
Free educational resources on security, scams, and financial topics
cons: Limited geographic footprint (St. Louis metro only) with no online-only or nationwide banking option
Website does not disclose interest rates for standard checking or savings accounts
No information about minimum balance requirements, overdraft fees, or other account fees
Limited details on loan products beyond mortgages, making it unclear what small business or personal loan options exist
Minimal information about mobile banking capabilities or digital-first features
best_for: St. Louis metro residents seeking relationship-based community banking with local branch access
Homebuyers and refinancers looking for mortgage services from an established local lender
Savers interested in competitive CD rates with personalized service
diagnosis: Lindell Bank is best for St. Louis metro area residents who value personal service, local market knowledge, and relationship banking from an established, stable community institution. The main caveat is that this bank has no online-only option or presence outside the St. Louis region, making it unsuitable for customers outside their service area or those preferring digital-first banking without branch relationships.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
